qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe Java deserialization in RMI context propagation allows remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the exact same vulnerable code/root cause as the advisory: ContextPayload.read() at line 47 calls oi.readObject() on untrusted RMI stream input without any deserialization filter. This is the identical vulnerable component (OpenTelemetry RMI instrumentation), same vulnerability class (CWE-502 unsafe deserialization), same attack vector (network access to RMI port), and same impact (RCE via gadget chains).

selected_finding.title: Unsafe Java deserialization in RMI context propagation allows remote code execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding perfectly matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The PoC successfully executed and demonstrated exploit success: a deserialization gadget chain executed arbitrary code during readObject(), creating the RCE marker file. The exploit satisfied the exploit_success_predicate by proving that untrusted serialized data reaches the vulnerable oi.readObject() sink and achieves remote code execution via gadget chains.
evidence: The finding identifies the exact vulnerable code location: ContextPayload.java:47 with oi.readObject()
The @SuppressWarnings("BanSerializableRead") annotation confirms the developers were aware of the deserialization risk but suppressed the warning
The PoC successfully created a serialized gadget object and demonstrated it executes during deserialization
The RCE marker file /tmp/evohunt-rce-marker was created during readObject(), proving arbitrary code execution
The exploit path matches the advisory: network -> RMI port -> JRMP call -> ContextPayload.read() -> oi.readObject() -> gadget execution

poc_executions.observed_effect: The PoC successfully executed the deserialization attack. The vulnerable code pattern (oi.readObject() without filter) was confirmed in ContextPayload.java. A serialized gadget was created and deserialized through the same readObject() path used by the vulnerable method. The gadget's readObject() callback executed and created the marker file /tmp/evohunt-rce-marker with proof-of-execution details (username: mac, timestamp: Mon Jun 08 17:23:54 CST 2026). This demonstrates complete end-to-end RCE through the advisory-described vulnerability.
